title: DAY 16 — CRM-M11 — SOCIAL MEDIA OPERATIONS (INSTAGRAM / FACEBOOK / YOUTUBE / REVIEWS)

questions.question: What are the three parallel social media tracks that must be completed daily, and which tool is used for each?

options.text: BIK for IG/FB, Shopify Judge.me for reviews, YouTube Studio for YouTube comments
Instagram app for IG, Facebook Business Suite for FB, Metricool for YouTube
BIK for IG/FB comments and DMs, Judge.me (via Shopify) for product reviews, Metricool for YouTube comments
Hootsuite for all social channels, Judge.me for reviews, Slack for escalations

questions.question: What is the correct daily priority order for social media tasks?

options.text: Judge.me reviews → BIK comments → BIK DMs → Metricool YouTube
BIK DMs → BIK IG/FB comments → Judge.me review management → Metricool YouTube
Metricool YouTube → BIK DMs → Judge.me reviews → BIK comments
BIK IG/FB comments → BIK DMs → Metricool YouTube → Judge.me reviews

questions.question: An executive wants to reply to an Instagram comment directly from the Instagram app to save time. Is this correct?

options.text: Yes — using the native app is faster and equally acceptable
No — all IG/FB comments and DMs must be handled through BIK; native apps must not be used for comment management
Yes — native apps are preferred for DMs; BIK is only for comments
No — but YouTube comments can be handled directly in YouTube Studio

questions.question: The Comment Reply Format.xlsx has three tabs. Which tab is the authoritative source for reply templates?

options.text: Comment Reply tab — the original and most complete version
Modified Comment Reply tab — this supersedes the original tab and must be used for all replies
Offline Store Req. tab — contains all templates organised by store location
Any tab can be used — they contain the same templates

questions.question: A customer comments on an Instagram post: "I received a damaged watch, the glass is cracked." Which template category should the executive use?

options.text: A-11 (Return & Refund) — customer needs a return
A-04 (Damage / Defective / Bad Quality) — acknowledge, apologise, direct to WhatsApp/call for resolution
A-37 (Poor Service / Negative Experience) — customer is expressing general dissatisfaction
A-39 (Complaint Escalation) — direct them to email customercare@sylvi.in

questions.question: A customer DMs on Facebook asking about tracking their order. The DM includes an order ID. What should the executive do before replying?

options.text: Reply with template A-18 (Track Order) immediately — no verification needed
Verify the order in FIMS → OMS → Shopify, then provide the delivery status using a customised reply based on the actual order data
Ask the customer to call +91-9687660234 for tracking information
Forward the DM to the logistics team via Slack

questions.question: What is the non-negotiable rule about template placeholders?

options.text: Placeholders should be replaced when possible but can be left as-is if the information is unavailable
Never send a reply with unfilled placeholders — if [Order ID] or [Name] is left blank, it is a quality failure
Placeholders are only relevant for email templates, not social media replies
Placeholders should be removed entirely if the data is not available

questions.question: A customer asks on Instagram: "Why is the price different on Amazon versus your website?" Which template should be used?

options.text: A-13 (Offers & Discounts) — direct them to the offers page
A-01 (Price or More Details) — send pricing details via DM
A-26 (Price Difference Across Platforms) — explain that prices vary due to service levels and associated expenses
A-36 (Prototype Price Increase) — explain the pricing transition

questions.question: In Judge.me review management, which reviews should be published on the Shopify storefront?

options.text: All reviews rated 3 stars and above
All reviews rated 4 stars and above
Only reviews rated 5 stars — all reviews rated 4 stars or below must be hidden
All reviews regardless of rating — transparency is required

questions.question: A new review is rated 5 stars but the text says "Looks good but the strap broke after one week." What should the executive do?

options.text: Publish it — 5-star rating qualifies for publication regardless of text
Hide it — the text is negative, so it should not be shown
Escalate to Asst Manager before publishing — the text contradicts the star rating (borderline case)
Delete it — contradictory reviews should be removed

questions.question: When hiding a 3-star review in Judge.me, should the review be deleted?

options.text: Yes — negative reviews should be permanently removed
No — hide only; do not delete reviews; hiding protects brand image while preserving the data
Only delete if the review contains abusive language
Move it to an archive tab for future reference

questions.question: Where should YouTube comments be monitored and replied to?

options.text: YouTube Studio — the native platform for comment management
Metricool Dashboard — Inbox → YouTube channel filter; replies are posted within Metricool, not YouTube Studio
BIK Dashboard — the same tool used for IG/FB comments
Hootsuite — the centralised social media management platform

questions.question: What are the three columns in the Daily Reporting Performance Ana sheet that track social media resolution, and what is the EOD target?

options.text: Resolved IG / Resolved FB / Resolved YT — target: count of resolved items
Pending Social Media IG / Pending Social Media FB / Pending Social Media YT — target: all three = 0 by EOD
Total Comments IG / Total Comments FB / Total Comments YT — target: 100% reply rate
Open IG / Open FB / Open YT — target: all three = 0 by shift start

questions.question: A customer DMs on Instagram asking about a physical store. What are the 4 fields that must be logged in the Offline Store Req. tab?

options.text: Order ID, Customer Name, Phone, Platform
Name, Email, Contact Number, Source (platform they came from)
Name, Address, City, Pincode
Customer Name, Query Type, Resolution, Date

questions.question: A customer threatens legal action against Sylvi in a public Instagram comment. What is the correct escalation?

options.text: Reply publicly with template A-39 (Complaint Escalation) and close the comment
Do not engage further; screenshot the comment and report to Asst Manager → Manager immediately
Delete the comment to prevent public visibility, then inform Asst Manager
Reply with an apology and offer a refund to de-escalate

questions.question: A YouTube comment mentions a product safety concern ("the watch battery started leaking"). What is the correct escalation?

options.text: Reply with template A-04 (Damage/Defective) and close the comment
Escalate to Asst Manager via `customer-care` Slack for quality review
Flag immediately to Manager via Slack `after-sale-services`
Ask the customer to share a video and wait for their response before escalating
